task: Add v4 → v5 save migration that synthesizes copper_ore_sample for saves soft-locked on bridge_collapse step 6.

migration_registry_pattern.shape: chained guards in loadGame() of the form `if (migrated.version === N) migrated = migrateNtoNplus1(migrated);`
migration_registry_pattern.keying_convention: registry keys are 'from version' — guard tests current version, function name reads `migrate<from>to<to>`
migration_registry_pattern.version_constant: CURRENT_VERSION at top of file (was 4, now 5); defaultSave() reads from this constant so new saves auto-pickup v5

spec_compliance_notes.stepIndex_correctness: Used stepIndex === 5 because the bug report describes 'step 6' but stepIndex is 0-indexed, matching the convention of other game state fields.
spec_compliance_notes.activeQuest_null_safety: Used `aq && aq.id === 'bridge_collapse'` form. Saves with `activeQuest: null` (the default) short-circuit on the first conjunct and pass through with only the version bump.
spec_compliance_notes.version_bump_always_applied: The function always returns `{ ...data, version: 5 }` even when soft-lock pattern doesn't match. This is required so the chained `if (migrated.version === 4)` gate stops on the next pass and so the merged save in loadGame correctly carries forward.
spec_compliance_notes.defaultSave_at_v5: defaultSave() returns `version: CURRENT_VERSION`, which is now 5 — new games start at v5 automatically with no separate change needed.
spec_compliance_notes.merged_overwrite_safe: loadGame's `merged = { ...defaultSave(), ...migrated, version: CURRENT_VERSION }` final assignment forces version to CURRENT_VERSION; harmless given the migration already produced v5, and provides defense-in-depth.

idempotency_proof.claim: Running migrateV4toV5 twice on the same v4 save (or once on a v5 save that was previously migrated) produces an equivalent state.

case_1_no_active_quest.first_run: matchesSoftLock = false (aq is null) → returns { ...data, version: 5 }
case_1_no_active_quest.second_run_on_output: matchesSoftLock = false (aq is null) → returns { ...data, version: 5 } — identical

case_2_different_quest.first_run: matchesSoftLock = false (aq.id !== 'bridge_collapse') → returns { ...data, version: 5 } unchanged inventory

case_3_completed_quest.first_run: matchesSoftLock = false (aq is null) → returns { ...data, version: 5 }

case_4_soft_lock_match_first_application.second_run_on_output: hasCanonical is now true → matchesSoftLock = false → returns { ...data, version: 5 } — inventory unchanged from first run's output. STABLE.

case_5_already_has_canonical.first_run: hasCanonical = true → matchesSoftLock = false → returns { ...data, version: 5 } unchanged inventory

idempotency_proof.conclusion: All five cases reach a fixed point on the first invocation. Subsequent invocations are no-ops because the canonical item is now present (case 4) or the gate already returns false (cases 1-3, 5).

preservation_of_existing_saves.completed_bridge_collapse: If 'bridge_collapse' is in completedQuests and activeQuest is null/different, the migration is a no-op aside from the version bump — guard short-circuits on aq.id check.
preservation_of_existing_saves.different_active_quest: Any activeQuest with id !== 'bridge_collapse' or stepIndex !== 5 passes through unchanged.
preservation_of_existing_saves.no_mine_copper: If inventory lacks both surface_copper and deep_copper, hasMineCopper is false and the gate fails — pass through.
preservation_of_existing_saves.already_has_canonical: If copper_ore_sample is already in inventory, hasCanonical is true and the gate fails — pass through (no duplicate).

build_status.notes: Only pre-existing warnings (CJS deprecation, chunk size > 500kB on vendor bundles) — unrelated to this change.
